title: A plea for the rule of law
primary_topics: motion for reconsideration as path to revision
rule of law and acceptance of judicial outcomes
sub_topics: stock and transfer book as primary corporate record
La Bugal-B'laan v. Ramos (January 27, 2004 → December 1, 2004 reversal)

stances.claim: The Supreme Court's 13-0-2 decision declaring the Sara Duterte impeachment unconstitutional is not yet final — the OSG has filed an MR, and the Court should explain its position on three serious issues: alleged factual errors about the one-year bar, the self-serving effect of making it nearly impossible to impeach high officials, and the operative-fact doctrine.
stances.rhetorical_move: structured three-issue critique + reminder that finality has not attached

stances.claim: Reversal on MR is possible even after unanimous decisions — Lopez v. Lopez (April 2025) and his own La Bugal-B'laan v. Ramos (2004) both demonstrate that the Court can completely reverse itself after MR, including via oral argument and memoranda.
stances.rhetorical_move: self-citation of his own ponencia + comparative precedent for reversal

stances.claim: The Court should set the impeachment case for oral argument and require memoranda — 'if the Court had patiently heard oral argument on less important problems like the recognition of foreign divorces and the PhilHealth petitions, why not on this monumental case?'

Once all due process procedures are undertaken, everyone should respect and accept any decision the justices may promulgate — whether we agree with it or not. That is simply the rule of law in our democracy.

events: Sara Duterte impeachment filing and Senate archiving
His main dissent (January 2004) and majority resolution authorship (December 2004) in La Bugal-B'laan

signature_phrases: All I humbly ask—and expect—is their openness to rethink and to change their stance if convinced by their conscience
yours truly writing the main dissent
the rule of law in our democracy
register_markers: HOWEVER, THE DECISION IS NOT YET FINAL
self-quotation of his prior public statement
decision_framework_signals: applies MR-can-reverse-unanimity principle as procedural-doctrinal opening for revision
uses self-citation of his own La Bugal-B'laan reversal authorship as authoritative precedent
applies procedural-completeness norm (oral argument + memoranda) as test of legitimate decision-making
frames rule-of-law as acceptance of due-process outcomes regardless of substantive agreement
cross_references: As noted in my public statement published and broadcast on July 27
notable_anecdotes: Writing the main dissent in La Bugal-B'laan v. Ramos (January 27, 2004) and then writing the majority resolution reversing the Court (December 1, 2004) — a personal example of MR reversal
